claim: 7 World Trade Center, which collapsed at 5:20 p.m. on September 11, 2001, housed: the SEC's New York field office (with files for several hundred active investigations including post-IPO cases involving major brokerage firms — estimates of total active-case files lost range from ~3,000 to ~4,000); the Secret Service's largest field office (whose investigative case files were destroyed, per a Secret Service agent quoted as saying 'All the evidence that we stored at 7 World Trade, in all our cases, went down with the building'); IRS regional offices; and Salomon Smith Barney (a Citigroup subsidiary), whose later-subpoenaed emails relevant to the WorldCom investigation were lost in the collapse.

sources.label: SEC Historical Society — “The Enforcement Division: A History — 9/11 and Enron Bring New Resources”
sources.quote: its New York offices at 7 World Trade Center were destroyed in the attacks

sources.label: CBS News — “Mystery surrounds loss of records, art on 9/11”
ABC News — “Law Enforcement Lost Evidence in Rubble”
Wikipedia — “List of tenants in 7 World Trade Center (1987–2001)”
Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: SEC Historical Society source is institutionally-primary and authoritative on the SEC enforcement-files loss. ABC News and CBS News carry the contemporaneous law-enforcement quotes. The reel implies the records destruction was the operational reason for WTC 7's destruction; the records-loss is documented but the causal inference is unsupported.
